title: Pyrus trilobata
intro: Pyrus trilobata, also known as Asian pear or Chinese pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species native to East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus trilobata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as tinctures and capsules, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of the common cold.
taxonomy: Pyrus trilobata, also known as the three-lobed pear or Siberian pear, belongs to the family Rosaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as the rose family. Pyrus trilobata is further classified under the genus Pyrus, which comprises various species of pear trees. The genus Pyrus is classified under the order Rosales, which includes other flowering plants like roses and raspberries. The Rosaceae family is part of the larger class Magnoliopsida, which encompasses a wide range of flowering plants.
